memberdef.name: SHRINK
memberdef.initializer: if ((!PARSER_PROGRESSIVE(ctxt)) && \ (ctxt->input->cur - ctxt->input->base > 2 * INPUT_CHUNK) && \ (ctxt->input->end - ctxt->input->cur < 2 * INPUT_CHUNK)) \ xmlParserShrink(ctxt);

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML element, new version, non recursive.
Handle an out-of-memory error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlParserErrors error, const char *msg, const xmlChar *str1, const xmlChar *str2)
memberdef.name: htmlParseErr

briefdescription.para: Handle a fatal parser error, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: violating Well-Formedness constraints

briefdescription.para: Pushes a new element name on top of the name stack.

simplesect.para: -1 in case of error, the index in the stack otherwise

briefdescription.para: Pops the top element name from the name stack.

simplesect.para: the name just removed

briefdescription.para: Pushes a new element name on top of the node info stack.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of error, the index in the stack otherwise

briefdescription.para: Pops the top element name from the node info stack.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of error, the pointer to NodeInfo otherwise

briefdescription.para: Prescan to find encoding.

detaileddescription.para: Try to find an encoding in the current data available in the input buffer.
TODO: Implement HTML5 prescan algorithm.

simplesect.para: an encoding string or NULL if not found

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, const xmlChar *str, size_t len, int partial)
memberdef.name: htmlValidateUtf8

briefdescription.para: skip all blanks character found at that point in the input streams.

simplesect.para: the number of space chars skipped

briefdescription.para: Lookup the HTML tag in the ElementTable.

simplesect.para: the related htmlElemDesc or NULL if not found.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the element to look up the priority for.

simplesect.para: value: The "endtag" priority.

briefdescription.para: Checks whether the new tag is one of the registered valid tags for closing old.

simplesect.para: 0 if no, 1 if yes.

briefdescription.para: The HTML DTD allows an ending tag to implicitly close other tags.
Close all remaining tags at the end of the stream.
The HTML DTD allows a tag to implicitly close other tags.

detaileddescription.para: The list is kept in htmlStartClose array. This function is called when a new tag has been detected and generates the appropriates closes if possible/needed. If newtag is NULL this mean we are at the end of the resource and we should check

parameterdescription.para: The new tag name or NULL

briefdescription.para: The HTML DTD allows a tag to implicitly close other tags.

detaileddescription.para: The list is kept in htmlStartClose array. This function checks if the element or one of it's children would autoclose the given tag.

simplesect.para: 1 if autoclose, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: The HTML DTD allows a tag to implicitly close other tags.

detaileddescription.para: The list is kept in htmlStartClose array. This function checks if a tag is autoclosed by one of it's child

simplesect.para: 1 if autoclosed, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: The HTML DTD allows a tag to exists only implicitly called when a new tag has been detected and generates the appropriates implicit tags if missing.
Prepare for non-whitespace character data.
Check if an attribute is of content type Script.

simplesect.para: 1 is the attribute is a script 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Lookup the given entity in EntitiesTable.

detaileddescription.para: TODO: the linear scan is really ugly, an hash table is really needed.

simplesect.para: the associated htmlEntityDesc if found, NULL otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Lookup the given entity in EntitiesTable.

detaileddescription.para: TODO: the linear scan is really ugly, an hash table is really needed.

simplesect.para: the associated htmlEntityDesc if found, NULL otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned char *out, int *outlen, const unsigned char *in, int *inlen)
memberdef.name: htmlUTF8ToHtml

briefdescription.para: Take a block of UTF-8 chars in and try to convert it to an ASCII plus HTML entities block of chars out.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of bytes to store the result
a pointer to an array of UTF-8 chars

simplesect.para: 0 if success, -2 if the transcoding fails, or -1 otherwise The value of inlen after return is the number of octets consumed as the return value is positive, else unpredictable. The value of outlen after return is the number of octets consumed.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned char *out, int *outlen, const unsigned char *in, int *inlen, int quoteChar)
memberdef.name: htmlEncodeEntities

briefdescription.para: Take a block of UTF-8 chars in and try to convert it to an ASCII plus HTML entities block of chars out.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of bytes to store the result
a pointer to an array of UTF-8 chars
the quote character to escape (' or ") or zero.

simplesect.para: 0 if success, -2 if the transcoding fails, or -1 otherwise The value of inlen after return is the number of octets consumed as the return value is positive, else unpredictable. The value of outlen after return is the number of octets consumed.

briefdescription.para: Is this a sequence of blank chars that one can ignore ?

simplesect.para: 1 if ignorable 0 if whitespace, -1 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Creates a new HTML document without a DTD node if URI and publicId are NULL.

simplesect.para: a new document, do not initialize the DTD if not provided

briefdescription.para: Creates a new HTML document.

simplesect.para: a new document

briefdescription.para: parse an HTML tag or attribute name, note that we convert it to lowercase since HTML names are not case-sensitive.

parameterdescription.para: whether this is an attribute name

simplesect.para: the Tag Name parsed or NULL

memberdef.argsstring: (const xmlChar *string, size_t slen, int isAttr, int *nlen, int *rlen)
memberdef.name: htmlFindEntityPrefix

memberdef.argsstring: (htmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, htmlAsciiMask mask, int comment, int refs, int maxLength)
memberdef.name: htmlParseData

briefdescription.para: Parse data until terminator is reached.

simplesect.para: the parsed string or NULL in case of errors.

parameterdescription.para: location to store the entity name

simplesect.para: NULL.

briefdescription.para: parse a value for an attribute Note: the parser won't do substitution of entities here, this will be handled later in xmlStringGetNodeList, unless it was asked for ctxt->replaceEntities != 0

simplesect.para: the AttValue parsed or NULL.

memberdef.argsstring: (htmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, const xmlChar *buf, int size, int mode)
memberdef.name: htmlCharDataSAXCallback

briefdescription.para: Parse character data and references.

parameterdescription.para: true if the input buffer is incomplete

simplesect.para: 1 if all data was parsed, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML comment.

parameterdescription.para: true if this is a bogus comment

briefdescription.para: Parse a DOCTYPE SYTSTEM or PUBLIC literal.

simplesect.para: the literal or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Parse a DOCTYPE declaration.
parse an attribute

detaileddescription.para: [41] Attribute ::= Name Eq AttValue
With namespace:
[NS 11] Attribute ::= QName Eq AttValue
Also the case QName == xmlns:??? is handled independently as a namespace definition.

parameterdescription.para: a xmlChar ** used to store the value of the attribute

simplesect.para: the attribute name, and the value in *value.

briefdescription.para: Handle charset encoding in meta tag.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, unsigned size, const xmlChar *name, unsigned hashValue, int aindex)
memberdef.name: htmlAttrHashInsert

briefdescription.para: Inserts a new attribute into the hash table.

parameterdescription.para: attribute index (this is a multiple of 5)

simplesect.para: INT_MAX if no existing attribute was found, the attribute index if an attribute was found, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: parse a start of tag either for rule element or EmptyElement.

detaileddescription.para: In both case we don't parse the tag closing chars.
[40] STag ::= '<' Name (S Attribute)* S? '>'
[44] EmptyElemTag ::= '<' Name (S Attribute)* S? '/>'
With namespace:
[NS 8] STag ::= '<' QName (S Attribute)* S? '>'
[NS 10] EmptyElement ::= '<' QName (S Attribute)* S? '/>'

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 in case of error and 1 if discarded

briefdescription.para: parse an end of tag

detaileddescription.para: With namespace
[NS 9] ETag ::= '</' QName S? '>'

simplesect.para: 1 if the current level should be closed.

briefdescription.para: Parse a content: comment, sub-element, reference or text.

detaileddescription.para: New version for non recursive htmlParseElementInternal

briefdescription.para: This is kept for compatibility with previous code versions.

simplesect.para: a node list.

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML document and invoke the SAX handlers.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful if you're only interested in custom SAX callbacks. If you want a document tree, use htmlCtxtParseDocument.

simplesect.para: 0, -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a parser context.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Free all the memory used by a parser context.

detaileddescription.para: However the parsed document in ctxt->myDoc is not freed.

briefdescription.para: Allocate and initialize a new HTML parser context.

detaileddescription.para: This can be used to parse HTML documents into DOM trees with functions like xmlCtxtReadFile or xmlCtxtReadMemory.
See htmlCtxtUseOptions for parser options.
See xmlCtxtSetErrorHandler for advanced error handling.
See htmlNewSAXParserCtxt for custom SAX parsers.

simplesect.para: the htmlParserCtxt or NULL in case of allocation error

briefdescription.para: Allocate and initialize a new HTML SAX parser context.

detaileddescription.para: If userData is NULL, the parser context will be passed as user data.

2.11.0
If you want support older versions, it's best to invoke htmlNewParserCtxt and set ctxt->sax with struct assignment.
Also see htmlNewParserCtxt.

simplesect.para: the htmlParserCtxt or NULL in case of allocation error

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *url, const char *buffer, size_t size, const char *encoding)
memberdef.name: htmlCreateMemoryParserCtxtInternal

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context for an HTML in-memory document.

detaileddescription.para: The input buffer must not contain any terminating null bytes.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context for a null-terminated string.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of xmlChar

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Check whether there's enough data in the input buffer to finish parsing a tag.

detaileddescription.para: This has to take quotes into account.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxtPtr ctxt, size_t startDelta, const char *str, size_t strLen, size_t extraLen)
memberdef.name: htmlParseLookupString

briefdescription.para: Check whether the input buffer contains a string.

parameterdescription.para: delta to apply at the start

briefdescription.para: Try to find a comment end tag in the input stream The search includes "-->" as well as WHATWG-recommended incorrectly-closed tags.

simplesect.para: the index to the current parsing point if the full sequence is available, -1 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Try to progress on parsing.

simplesect.para: zero if no parsing was possible

memberdef.argsstring: (htmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *chunk, int size, int terminate)
memberdef.name: htmlParseChunk

briefdescription.para: Parse a chunk of memory in push parser mode.

detaileddescription.para: Assumes that the parser context was initialized with htmlCreatePushParserCtxt.

The last chunk, which will often be empty, must be marked with the terminate flag. With the default SAX callbacks, the resulting document will be available in ctxt->myDoc. This pointer will not be freed by the library.
If the document isn't well-formed, ctxt->myDoc is set to NULL.
Since 2.14.0, xmlCtxtGetDocument can be used to retrieve the result document.

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code (0 on success).

memberdef.argsstring: (htmlSAXHandler *sax, void *user_data, const char *chunk, int size, const char *filename, xmlCharEncoding enc)
memberdef.name: htmlCreatePushParserCtxt

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context for using the HTML parser in push mode.

parameterdescription.para: The user data returned on SAX callbacks (optional)
a pointer to an array of chars (optional)
number of chars in the array

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML in-memory document.

detaileddescription.para: If sax is not NULL, use the SAX callbacks to handle parse events. If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM behavior and return a tree.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of xmlChar
a free form C string describing the HTML document encoding, or NULL
if using SAX, this pointer will be provided on callbacks.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree unless SAX is NULL or the document is not well formed.

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses deprecated global parser options.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of xmlChar

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context to read from a file.

detaileddescription.para: A non-NULL encoding overrides encoding declarations in the document.
Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time.

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: parse an HTML file and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time. It use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback. If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.

parameterdescription.para: if using SAX, this pointer will be provided on callbacks.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree unless SAX is NULL or the document is not well formed.

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML file and build a tree.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Set and return the previous value for handling HTML omitted tags.

simplesect.para: the last value for 0 for no handling, 1 for auto insertion.
HTML_VALID
HTML_VALID

parameterdescription.para: whether to allow deprecated elements (YES is faster here for Element nodes)

simplesect.para: HTML_VALID

briefdescription.para: Reset a parser context.

detaileddescription.para: Same as xmlCtxtReset.

briefdescription.para: Applies the options to the parser context.

detaileddescription.para: Unset options are cleared.

2.14.0
With older versions, you can use htmlCtxtUseOptions.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, the set of unknown or unimplemented options in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Applies the options to the parser context.

detaileddescription.para: The following options are never cleared and can only be enabled:

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, the set of unknown or unimplemented options in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML document and return the resulting document tree.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree or NULL

memberdef.argsstring: (const xmlChar *str, const char *url, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: htmlReadDoc

briefdescription.para: Convenience function to parse an HTML document from a zero-terminated string.

detaileddescription.para: See htmlCtxtReadDoc for details.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a zero terminated string

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree.

briefdescription.para: Convenience function to parse an HTML file from the filesystem, the network or a global user-defined resource loader.

detaileddescription.para: See htmlCtxtReadFile for details.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *buffer, int size, const char *url, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: htmlReadMemory

briefdescription.para: Convenience function to parse an HTML document from memory.

detaileddescription.para: The input buffer must not contain any terminating null bytes.
See htmlCtxtReadMemory for details.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (int fd, const char *url, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: htmlReadFd

briefdescription.para: Convenience function to parse an HTML document from a file descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: NOTE that the file descriptor will not be closed when the context is freed or reset.
See htmlCtxtReadFd for details.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlInputReadCallback ioread, xmlInputCloseCallback ioclose, void *ioctx, const char *url, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: htmlReadIO

briefdescription.para: Convenience function to parse an HTML document from I/O functions and context.

detaileddescription.para: See htmlCtxtReadIO for details.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const xmlChar *str, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: htmlCtxtReadDoc

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: See htmlCtxtUseOptions for details.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a zero terminated string

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *filename, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: htmlCtxtReadFile

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML file from the filesystem, the network or a user-defined resource loader.

detaileddescription.para: See htmlCtxtUseOptions for details.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *buffer, int size, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: htmlCtxtReadMemory

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: The input buffer must not contain any terminating null bytes.
See htmlCtxtUseOptions for details.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, int fd, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: htmlCtxtReadFd

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML from a file descriptor and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: See htmlCtxtUseOptions for details.
NOTE that the file descriptor will not be closed when the context is freed or reset.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, xmlInputReadCallback ioread, xmlInputCloseCallback ioclose, void *ioctx, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: htmlCtxtReadIO

briefdescription.para: Parse an HTML document from I/O functions and source and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: See htmlCtxtUseOptions for details.

parameterdescription.para: the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree
